title: 3.2.1 Pattern Matching: Definition and Applications
subtitle: Core concept, typical scenarios, and extension-ready visualization/code/practice

content.introduction: Pattern matching identifies occurrences of a pattern in text and is foundational for search, filtering, and language processing.

sections.title: 3.2.1.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define text, pattern, exact match, first occurrence, and all occurrences. Clarify why naive scanning may become expensive on long repetitive inputs.
examples: Distinguish exact matching from fuzzy matching
Explain why repeated prefixes affect performance

sections.title: 3.2.1.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe the general matching pipeline: preprocessing assumptions, scan window movement, mismatch handling, and result reporting.
examples: Scan a log line sequence for fixed signatures
Show mismatch handling with a moving window

sections.title: 3.2.1.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Compare baseline and optimized strategies at a high level, including when to move from simple matching to advanced algorithms.
examples: Use brute force for small one-off inputs
Use preprocessed methods for long repetitive text
Escalate to multi-pattern matching in IDS pipelines

keyTakeaways: Recognize input characteristics that affect speed
Choose suitable matching strategy by workload
Connect matching concepts to real engineering systems
